cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Steven could have gone to the gas station. The key here is to look at all the times he was seen doing other activities and find the gaps when he wasn't accounted for. Also, we need to remember that the gas station is closed after 10pm, so he can't have gone there after that. First, let's list out all the known activities with their times: - Steven woke up at 7am. So he's up from 7am onwards. But this doesn't necessarily mean he went somewhere right away. - Ashley saw Steven stretching at a yoga studio from 7am to 10am. So he was there the entire time between 7 and 10 am. - William saw Steven reading at the library from 1pm to 2pm. - Jennifer saw Steven working at the office from 2pm to 3pm. - Michael saw Steven watching a movie at the theater from 3pm to 7pm. - Mary saw Steven waiting at the train station from 7pm to 10pm. So let's plot these on a timeline: - 7am to 10am: Yoga studio - 1pm to 2pm: Library - 2pm to 3pm: Office - 3pm to 7pm: Movie theater - 7pm to 10pm: Train station Now, looking for the gaps between these times. From 7am to 10am: occupied with yoga. Then, the next activity is at 1pm. So between 10am and 1pm, there's a gap. That's 10am to 1pm. During this time, Steven isn't accounted for. So he could have gone to the gas station during this time. Then from 1pm to 2pm: library. Then from 2pm to 3pm: office. Then
